LOCAL GOVERNMENT ROUNDUP

Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee advanced an updated nuisance ordinance and pushed for a county traffic study this week, while several other area boards met with outcomes still pending.

  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee unanimously forwarded revised Chapter 382 nuisance ordinance language to the Village Board for final approval after a public hearing July 6.
  • The committee also reached consensus to ask Marathon County to conduct a traffic study at County Road XX and Terabonding Drive, with the existing four-way stop staying in place in the meantime.
  • Staff were directed to research costs and grant options for a bike and pedestrian path along Highway X and report back to the committee in August.
  • The Kronenwetter Village Board is set to consider ballot drop box installation and proposed revisions to its elections chapter at its July 13 meeting.
  • The Wausau School Board is set to act on Wisconsin Academic Standards adoption, WIAA membership renewals for East, West, and WAVE, and an EFIS wall repair at South Mountain Elementary.

Coming up: The Wausau Common Council meets Tuesday at 6:30 p.m., preceded by Finance Committee at 5:15 p.m. and Board of Public Works at 10:30 a.m.; Marathon County's Public Safety Committee also convenes Tuesday at 5:00 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
